title: Roastery — coffee that earns the time you give it

blocks.headline: Coffee that earns the time you give it.
blocks.subheadline: A small roastery on the corner of Maple and Third. We pull twelve shots before opening and write the day's tasting notes by hand.

left.heading: We started with one machine and a stubborn opinion.
left.text: In 2018 we leased a narrow storefront with a sticky door and put a single drum roaster in the back. The opinion: most coffee in this neighborhood was either stale or trying too hard. We thought we could do something quieter and better. Six years on, the door still sticks.

right.text: Today we roast three lots a week — small enough to taste every batch, large enough to keep the bar moving. The cafe seats sixteen. The espresso machine is a 1998 La Marzocco we rebuilt ourselves. The regulars know which Yirgacheffe just landed and they ask after it by name.

items.title: Konga · Yirgacheffe
items.description: Washed Ethiopia. Bergamot, jasmine, white peach. Light roast, intended for filter. Pulls beautifully as espresso if you prefer it bright.

items.title: Finca La Esperanza
items.description: Honey-process Colombia. Brown sugar, stone fruit, a long cocoa finish. Our default house espresso this month.

items.title: Sumatra Lintong
items.description: Wet-hulled Indonesia. Earthy, herbal, syrupy body. The one to order if you take milk and want it to stand up.

blocks.quote: I came in for a flat white and stayed for a forty-minute conversation about Ethiopian processing methods. They didn't make me feel stupid for asking.
blocks.author: Maya R.

blocks.title: Saturdays are slowest. The window seat is yours.
blocks.description: Doors at 7am Tuesday through Sunday. Closed Mondays — we use the day to roast.
